comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ every file listed in required_attachments. Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 121, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order, no numbered prefixes):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (3-5 sentences in your own words) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence for each WI-1..WI-4 — do NOT group) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new governance doc with exact path) D. FILES TO MODIFY (should be empty — this EA creates only) E. FILES TO READ (every production source file + ADR + STYLE.md + predecessor Sprint 9 EA-2 docs you will read for authoring context) F. NEGATIVE CONSTRAINTS YOU UNDERSTAND (enumerate NC-1..NC-8 from §5 in your own words — MUST list every NC individually) G. OPEN QUESTIONS (any ambiguity requiring SDO clarification) H. STRUCTURAL RECITATION (§ 7 line-by-line — verbatim section-header outline of the STYLE.md Doc Template, in document order) I. ANCHOR VERIFICATION (for each of the three docs, list primary source files and ADRs you will cite — flag any phantom substitutions)
If you cannot produce every section with substantive content, STOP and ask in the comprehension gate comment rather than guessing.
comprehension_gate.stop_condition: After posting the comprehension gate, STOP. Do not touch any file, do not branch, do not create docs. Wait for SDO to post [agent:sdo][phase:comprehension-review] VERDICT: APPROVED. If SDO returns REVISE, address feedback and re-post the gate (same prefix).

milestone.objective: Author three governance documents — context-spotlighting.md (GOV-08), session-state.md (GOV-09), and configuration-management.md (GOV-11) — covering the Assistant Orchestrator's operational state surface: how context is assembled and spotlighted for the model, how session state persists across AO lifecycle and UI reconnects, and how runtime configuration flows from TOML through validation into live service state. All three docs MUST conform to STYLE.md's seven-header Doc Template, clear the 150-line substantive floor, cite ≥ 1 ADR and ≥ 1 source file (closest-relevant where no direct ADR governs), and address the five audience personas (operator / developer / auditor / incident responder / future agent). Scope held strictly to docs/governance/** plus one ledger entry; zero production code modified (L-15).
milestone.sprint_context:
Sprint 9 governance docs rollout (Task 121, DEC-15 sprint_id=9). EA-1 authored the foundation set (STYLE.md, pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md). EA-2 added the runtime-resilience set (gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md). EA-3 (this milestone) completes the operational-state triangle: context → session → configuration. EA-4 then covers ops/deployment/rules, and EA-5 authors the README landing page.
Per Sprint 9 SDV, GOV-11 is the widest-scope doc in EA-3 because runtime configuration spans shared/runtime_config.py (schema), entrypoint.py (application), launcher/guest_deploy.py (artifact flow), and the 13 TOML constraints enumerated authoritatively in Sprint 8 EA-2 test_entrypoint.py. GOV-08 is narrowest (delimiter tokens, spotlight pipeline). GOV-09 sits in the middle (persistence + UI Gateway bridge).

work_item.title: docs/governance/context-spotlighting.md (GOV-08)
work_item.scope: New file. Authoritative governance doc for context assembly, delimiter tokens, spotlight pipeline, and prompt-boundary invariants that make PGOV stage-3 validation meaningful.
work_item.structure: STYLE.md seven-header Doc Template: # Context Spotlighting Governance ## Audience (all 5 personas addressed) ## Prerequisites (cite STYLE.md + pgov-validation.md + ADR-012) ## Source References (≥ 1 ADR + ≥ 1 source file) ## Governance Content (body — see key topics below) ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
work_item.key_topics: - Delimiter token taxonomy (system/user/assistant + PGOV sentinels) - Context assembly pipeline: prompt-builder → delimiter injection → tokenizer → model input - Spotlight invariants: which region is "trusted context" vs "untrusted user input" from PGOV's point of view - Stage-3 PGOV delimiter-integrity check as the enforcement point - Token budget accounting (delimiter overhead vs usable context) - Thinking-mode interaction (ADR-012 §2.4 — AO allows thinking; PA forces /no_think) as it relates to spotlight boundaries - Failure modes: delimiter collision, truncation mid-delimiter, nested assistant echoes; link each to the existing test that protects it
work_item.source_anchors: - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/context_manager.py — delimiter tokens, assembly pipeline - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py — stage-3 delimiter validation - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/constants.py — delimiter constant definitions (pinned values) - ADR-012 (Qwen3-14B Model Selection + Speculative Decoding) — thinking mode, tokenizer, prompt format
work_item.acceptance: - All seven STYLE.md headers present and populated - ≥ 1 ADR cited in Source References - ≥ 1 source file cited in Source References with precise symbol names - All five audience personas addressed in Audience section - Delimiter taxonomy enumerated (not just described) - Stage-3 PGOV link explicit (cross-ref pgov-validation.md §Stage-3)

work_item.title: docs/governance/session-state.md (GOV-09)
work_item.scope: New file. Authoritative governance doc for session identity lifecycle, persistence surface, UI Gateway bridging, and reconnect semantics.
work_item.structure: STYLE.md seven-header Doc Template (same as WI-1).
work_item.key_topics: - Session identity: how session_id is minted, scoped, and retired - Persistence surface: what survives across AO restart vs what is process-local - UI Gateway session_store bridge: session_panel.py (UI Shell) → ui_gateway session_store → AO context (session_id threading) - Reconnect semantics: UI reconnect on existing session_id vs fresh session - Session scope for PGOV / circuit breaker / context window (each keyed per-session or process-wide? cite code) - Failure modes: orphaned sessions, cross-session bleed-through, session_id collision
work_item.source_anchors: - services/ui_gateway/src/session_store.py — authoritative session persistence (SUBSTITUTE for phantom services/assistant_orchestrator/src/session.py) - services/ui_shell/src/session_panel.py — UI-side session lifecycle - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/context_manager.py — session_id threading through context assembly (cross-link with WI-1) - ADR-009 (Assistant-Interaction-Surface) — session contract between UI Shell, UI Gateway, and AO
work_item.adr_absence_note: No direct ADR governs SQLite session persistence (ADR-008 is about NPU concurrent scheduling, not sessions). Cite ADR-009 (closest-relevant, per STYLE.md Source-Anchoring rule) and note the ADR-absence in the doc's Open Questions section as a future ADR candidate.
work_item.acceptance: - All seven STYLE.md headers present - ≥ 1 ADR cited (ADR-009 expected) - ≥ 1 source file cited - All five audience personas addressed - Phantom substitution (session_store.py for session.py) explicitly noted in doc prose + ledger Notes section - UI Shell ↔ UI Gateway ↔ AO bridge traced end-to-end with source refs

work_item.title: docs/governance/configuration-management.md (GOV-11)
work_item.scope: New file. Authoritative governance doc for the runtime configuration flow: TOML schema → validation → service application, plus the 13 enumerated constraints and the Task 4 DEC-01..DEC-10 production decisions that they encode.
work_item.key_topics: - Configuration lifecycle: launcher reads TOML → validates → writes to VM → AO entrypoint loads → schema enforces → service applies - The 13 TOML constraints enumerated authoritatively by Sprint 8 EA-2 test_entrypoint.py::TestAssistantOrchestratorConfigValidation (device, priority, temperature, top_k, top_p, repetition_penalty, max_new_tokens, vsock_cid/port, timeout, max_message_bytes, PGOV threshold, response_depth_mode) - Task 4 DEC-01..DEC-10 production decisions the schema encodes - Fail-closed validation: invalid TOML → service refuses to start (cite entrypoint.py validation path) - Secret handling in config (if any) and what is host-only vs guest-facing - How config changes propagate: guest_deploy.py artifact flow, VM restart semantics
work_item.source_anchors: - shared/runtime_config.py — schema definition - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py — validation and application - launcher/guest_deploy.py — artifact flow to VM - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/constants.py — pinned defaults - services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_entrypoint.py (TestAssistantOrchestratorConfigValidation) — AUTHORITATIVE enumeration of the 13 constraints (cite by test name)
work_item.adr_absence_note: No direct ADR governs runtime configuration. Per STYLE.md's "closest-relevant" rule, cite the decision records that DO govern: DEC-01 through DEC-10 from Task 4 (production config decisions), explicitly in Prerequisites. Note ADR-absence in Open Questions as a candidate for a future ADR. This precedent was set by EA-2's circuit-breaker.md (which cited the circuit breaker's DEC-origin rather than a nonexistent ADR).
work_item.line_floor: ≥ 150 substantive lines (SDV flags this as the widest-scope doc in EA-3 — expect longer)
work_item.acceptance: - All seven STYLE.md headers present - Task 4 DEC-01..DEC-10 enumerated with one-line summary each - All 13 TOML constraints enumerated with valid-range + source-of-truth (test_entrypoint.py) - Fail-closed semantics explicit (service refuses to start) - Config propagation flow traced end-to-end (launcher → VM → AO) - ADR-absence documented in Open Questions - All five audience personas addressed

work_item.title: Ledger entry (Q1-1 per-file convention)
work_item.required_sections: - Frontmatter (ledger_id, date, sprint_id=9, entry_type=EA, predecessor=20260422_181301_sprint9_ea2_runtime-resilience, branch, merge_commit=null, disposition=COMPLETE) - ## Task 121 / EA-3: Operational State (heading) - ### Summary (3-5 sentence narrative) - ### Deliverables (table: WI | Artifact | Lines | GOV ID) - ### Files Changed (every file touched, with line counts) - ### Quality Gates (LINE-FLOOR, STYLE.md conformance, SOURCE-ANCHOR, L-15 / L-16 / L-17 / L-18 compliance) - ### Notes / Substitutions (phantom session.py → session_store.py, ADR-absence handling for GOV-09 + GOV-11)
work_item.acceptance: - Frontmatter complete - All three governance docs listed with line counts - All L-15..L-18 compliance items explicitly addressed - Phantom substitution + ADR-absence notes included

negative_constraints.nc: DO NOT modify any file under src/, services/*/src/, launcher/, shared/, tests/, or pyproject.toml. This milestone is docs-only (L-15).

DO NOT edit EA-1 docs (STYLE.md, pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md) or EA-2 docs (gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md). Cross-reference them via links — do NOT modify. If you discover an actual defect in an EA-1 or EA-2 doc, note it in the ledger's Notes section as a follow-up for SDO to triage; do not fix it inline (L-18 retroactive-edit prohibition).
DO NOT create or stub boot-sequence.md or any other phantom-referenced doc. Cite them as "(forthcoming / GOV-NN)" if cross-reference needed (L-17).

DO NOT duplicate content that already exists in EA-1 or EA-2 docs. If a topic is already governed by, e.g., pgov-validation.md, link to it instead of re-explaining. Exception: a one-paragraph recap is allowed where it is load-bearing for the reader's comprehension.
DO NOT create multiple ledger entries for this EA. One file only, following the Q1-1 per-file timestamp convention. DO NOT append to the frozen docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md.

DO NOT author new ADRs, decision records, or protocol specs. This EA is documentation of EXISTING behavior, not proposal of new behavior. If the authoring process reveals a design gap, record it in the doc's Open Questions / Deferred Items section.

DO NOT exceed scope: the three docs listed in §4 are the full deliverable set. No README tweaks, no governance/index updates, no touching EA-4 or EA-5 docs. EA-5 owns the README landing page — leave it untouched.
L-16 Sprint 8 coexistence: DO NOT touch anything under **/tests/ or anywhere else outside docs/governance/ + the single ledger file. Sprint 8 and Sprint 9 working sets are disjoint and must stay so.

risk.description: Phantom anchor: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/session.py named in continuation XML does NOT exist. EA could waste time searching, invent stubs, or worse — create the file (violating NC-1).
risk.resolution: Pre-approved substitution: services/ui_gateway/src/session_store.py is the authoritative session persistence surface. AO threads session_id opaquely through context_manager.py. Use session_store.py as the primary source anchor for GOV-09; note the substitution explicitly in both the doc prose (in Source References section) and the ledger's Notes / Substitutions section. This matches EA-2's precedent (gpu_inference.py ← model_loader.py; entrypoint.py + pgov.py ← error_handling.py).

risk.description: ADR-absence: no direct ADR governs session persistence (GOV-09) or runtime configuration (GOV-11). STYLE.md requires ≥ 1 ADR cited per doc. EA may freeze or invent an ADR.
risk.resolution: Per STYLE.md's "closest-relevant" rule: - GOV-09 → cite ADR-009 (Assistant-Interaction-Surface) which governs the UI Shell / UI Gateway / AO contract that session state rides on. Note ADR-absence for SQLite session persistence in the doc's Open Questions section. - GOV-11 → cite Task 4 DEC-01..DEC-10 as the governing decision records (they predate the ADR system's current use for config concerns). Note ADR-absence in Open Questions. EA-2's circuit-breaker.md set the precedent for citing DECs where no ADR exists. Do NOT author a new ADR (NC-6).

risk.description: Content duplication with EA-1 pgov-validation.md (stage-3 delimiter check is shared between GOV-08 and pgov-validation.md).
risk.resolution: GOV-08 owns the DELIMITER TAXONOMY and assembly pipeline. pgov-validation.md owns the VALIDATION mechanism. Cross-link bidirectionally. In GOV-08, the stage-3 mention should be one paragraph linking to pgov-validation.md § Stage-3, not a re-explanation. If uncertain, prefer under-explaining in GOV-08 and linking — duplication is the worse failure mode.

risk.description: Line-floor pressure: GOV-08 (narrowest scope) may struggle to clear 150 substantive lines.
risk.resolution: Fill substantively, not with padding: - Exhaustive delimiter-token enumeration (with pinned constant values) - Token budget worked example (delimiter overhead math) - Thinking-mode interaction (ADR-012 §2.4) - Failure-mode catalog with linked tests - Persona-by-persona guidance (5 audiences × short section each) If after substantive authoring the doc comes in at 140-149 lines, STOP and flag to SDO in the completion report — do NOT pad with filler. The 150-line floor targets substantive content; padding to meet it violates the spirit of the rule.

risk.description: Ledger convention drift: the EA queue prompt could name the frozen monolithic docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md as the ledger target (as it did for Sprint 9 EA-2).
risk.resolution: This prompt explicitly pins Q1-1 per-file convention. If any instruction elsewhere conflicts, follow docs/ledger/README.md (authoritative). EA-2's ledger entry flagged this drift for SDO follow-up — it remains unaddressed at authoring time. Do the right thing; flag again in Notes if you encounter it.

risk.description: The 13 TOML constraints are authoritatively enumerated in Sprint 8 EA-2 test_entrypoint.py::TestAssistantOrchestratorConfigValidation. If GOV-11 lists them independently from code inspection, the two sources may drift.
risk.resolution: Cite test_entrypoint.py as the authoritative enumeration by test name in GOV-11 Source References. Enumerate in the doc body, but mark the test as the ground truth: "If this list diverges from TestAssistantOrchestratorConfigValidation, the test wins — update this doc." This pattern is the standard across EA-2 docs.

risk.description: Parent-head drift: main may advance between authoring and EA pickup.
risk.resolution: EA MUST verify `git rev-parse HEAD` matches df686b8 before branching. If main has advanced, rebase onto current main. Do NOT force-checkout df686b8 (L-13). Predecessor ledger_id in frontmatter does not change.

risk.description: Audience section pro-forma: easy to write "operators should know X" in a way that doesn't actually address the persona. Audit oversight from a prior governance doc that's now referenced in STYLE.md.
risk.resolution: Each persona bullet must answer: "If you are [persona] and this doc is what you have, what specifically do you do / check / decide?" Not "what do you know." If a persona has no actionable guidance from this doc, say so explicitly rather than padding.

ea_prompt.structural_recitation:
DOC TEMPLATE (STYLE.md — all three docs MUST match):
# {Domain} Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites ## Source References ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
RECOVERY HEADER FLEX (per STYLE.md): Recovery / Remediation Procedures may be standalone OR internalized within Governance Content if the doc's subject matter IS recovery (not applicable to any EA-3 doc — keep standalone for all three).
AUDIENCE PERSONAS (STYLE.md §Audience Taxonomy): - Operator - Developer - Auditor - Incident Responder - Future Agent (Claude or successor)

gate.pass_criteria: Each doc ≥ 150 substantive lines (comment/blank lines subtracted). SDV expectation: GOV-11 largest, GOV-08 smallest but still ≥ 150.
Each file reports ≥ 6 level-2 headers (Audience, Prerequisites, Source References, Governance Content, Recovery / Remediation, Open Questions). Title (# ...) is level-1 and not counted here.

gate.command: grep -n "ADR-" docs/governance/context-spotlighting.md docs/governance/session-state.md docs/governance/configuration-management.md grep -n "\.py" docs/governance/context-spotlighting.md docs/governance/session-state.md docs/governance/configuration-management.md
gate.pass_criteria: Each doc has ≥ 1 ADR citation (or documented ADR-absence) and ≥ 1 source file citation.

Output contains ONLY: - docs/governance/context-spotlighting.md - docs/governance/session-state.md - docs/governance/configuration-management.md - docs/ledger/<timestamp>_sprint9_ea3_operational-state.md No src/, tests/, shared/, launcher/, services/ paths. No .py files. L-15 compliance is MACHINE-VERIFIED by this gate.
Output: 0. Zero test-file overlap with Sprint 8's working set.

verification.step: 1. Run all quality gates (§8). Paste output verbatim into completion report.
2. For each doc, perform STYLE.md cross-check: open STYLE.md and walk each Doc Template bullet against your doc. Any mismatch = RED.
3. Anchor verification: for each source file cited, open the file and confirm every symbol name referenced exists. Phantom citations = RED.
4. Audience persona check: for each of the five personas, find the sentence in each doc that addresses that persona. Missing = RED.

5. ADR-absence handling audit: for GOV-09 and GOV-11, confirm the ADR-absence is documented in BOTH Source References (citing closest- relevant instead) AND Open Questions (flagging the gap as a deferred item).
6. Cross-link audit: for each cross-reference to an EA-1 or EA-2 doc, confirm the target section header exists in the target file.

commit_template.staged_files: git add docs/governance/context-spotlighting.md git add docs/governance/session-state.md git add docs/governance/configuration-management.md git add docs/ledger/<timestamp>_sprint9_ea3_operational-state.md
commit_template.commit_message: [agent:ea_code] Sprint 9 EA-3: governance docs for operational state (GOV-08/09/11)

completion_report.required_sections: - Summary (3-5 sentences) - Deliverables table (WI | Artifact | Lines | GOV ID | Vikunja subtask) - Gate results (each of the five quality gates with command + output) - Phantom / ADR-absence handling (session.py → session_store.py; GOV-09 and GOV-11 ADR-absence citations) - Cross-references (predecessor ledger, SDV, continuation XML, this prompt) - Follow-ups (any discovered defects in EA-1/EA-2 docs; any gap flagged for future EAs)

ea_prompt.mature_not_minimal:
These docs are long-term governance artifacts that operators, auditors, and future agents will rely on for years. The 150-line floor is the minimum, not the target. Where the subject matter warrants depth — e.g., GOV-11's 13 constraints or GOV-09's lifecycle — author to the depth the subject deserves, not to the floor. The user's documented preference (memory: "mature Claude dev environment") is for polished, advanced, cohesive work — not minimum-viable wiring.
What polish looks like here: - Concrete worked examples (a config example, a delimiter layout illustration, a session-reconnect sequence diagram in prose) - Cross-links that actually resolve to sections that exist - Persona guidance that is actionable, not performative - Failure-mode catalogs linked to the tests that protect each mode - Explicit acknowledgement of what is NOT covered (deferred items)
What polish is NOT: - Padding to clear the line floor - Speculative future-feature discussion - Architectural opinion / proposal dressed as documentation - Re-explaining content from EA-1/EA-2 docs
